basics.name: Daniel L. Einars
basics.label: Lead Software Engineer
basics.summary: Lead Software Engineer focused on React and SvelteKit platforms, combining hands-on delivery with architecture leadership in regulated environments. I drive migrations, engineering standards, and maintainable user-facing systems in close collaboration with product, design, backend, and business stakeholders.

work.name: ti&m AG, Zurich, Switzerland
work.summary: Frontend Lead for a digital onboarding web application
highlights: Modernized a dormant React 15 onboarding platform to support identity verification, liveness checks, and KYC workflows under current security standards.
Integrated a onboarding product with existing custom implementations while resolving inconsistent APIs and undocumented third party dependencies.
Delivered alternative solutions and clear recommendations when customer requirements conflicted with platform constraints or compliance standards.
Upgraded the back office administration interface in parallel and documented near term technical debt to ensure stability before feature expansion.
Restored full platform functionality to meet compliance requirements while maintaining transparent expectations around post launch optimization.

work.name: ti&m AG, Zurich, Switzerland
work.summary: Frontend Lead for a large B2B e-banking application, combining hands-on delivery with technical leadership across architecture, standards, and cross-functional execution.
highlights: Set frontend technical direction aligned with business goals, platform constraints, and long-term maintainability for a regulated banking product.
Led delivery of complex user-facing features while improving code quality practices and team velocity.
Translated epics into implementable frontend solutions through close collaboration with product, design, and backend teams.
Put Renovate in place to keep dependencies current with less manual maintenance and lower upgrade risk.
Established type generation from external service contracts to cut integration errors and speed up frontend delivery.
Drove adoption of AI coding tools by aligning the team on guidelines, usage patterns, and supporting tooling to improve delivery without sacrificing consistency.

work.name: ti&m AG, Zürich, Switzerland
work.summary: Owned the B2C frontend for a large credit institution, leading platform modernization, delivery, and team enablement across a high-visibility user-facing product.
highlights: Migrated B2C frontend applications from create-react-app to Next.js, implementing incremental static regeneration to improve load times and reduce build durations.
Delivered frontend epics in teams of two to six engineers while balancing hands-on implementation with planning and technical guidance.
Aligned delivery decisions with business goals and user expectations through direct collaboration with client stakeholders.
Introduced automated linting rules that identified accessibility issues before code review, improving maintainability and reducing QA feedback loops.
Enabled junior engineers to independently own feature development through structured pair programming.

work.name: ti&m AG, Zurich, Switzerland
work.summary: Responsible for customer-facing React applications and Magnolia CMS support, with a focus on maintainability, tooling, and design-system-aligned frontend delivery.
highlights: Introduced ESLint, lint-staged, and Prettier across frontend repositories to standardize code quality and reduce manual review overhead.
Modernized local tooling and frontend dependencies to reduce environment inconsistencies and streamline developer onboarding.
Implemented site-wide design updates across React applications and Magnolia CMS in collaboration with design teams to ensure cross-channel consistency.

work.name: Noser Engineering AG, Winterthur, Switzerland
work.summary: Develop software solutions for clients of Noser Engineering AG
highlights: Built an Android remote-support application using WebRTC and Dagger 2, balancing real-time performance requirements with device resource constraints.
Led migration of 148 Ant projects to Gradle, reducing compile time from approximately 10 minutes to under 2 minutes.

work.name: BearingPoint AG, Zurich, Switzerland
highlights: Built web scrapers, data transformation tools, and automated test-case generation utilities for the EasyTax product.

work.name: TravelBird B.V. / GmbH, Zurich, Switzerland/Amsterdam, Netherlands
work.position: Marketing Manager Germany and Head of Marketing
highlights: Led performance-focused digital marketing initiatives across EU markets, including channel strategy, team leadership and ROAS optimization.

work.name: NEODAU GmbH and Co.KG, Hamburg, Germany
highlights: Managed partner channels, publisher relationships, client acquisition, and contract negotiation for online marketing programs.

education.area: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science ZHAW Zurich (BSc)

education.thesis: Analysing critical success factors of browser games

awards.title: Code-Night Hackathon
awards.summary: Contributed to a winning hackathon project developing a forgery-detection service capable of identifying Photoshop-manipulated images. Responsibilities included building the React SPA frontend, configuring nginx for network routing, and managing DevOps setup.

volunteer.summary: Active member of the volunteer fire brigade of Meggen, serving as a firefighter trained to operate self-contained breathing apparatus. This role reinforces disciplined execution under pressure and structured teamwork.
